about.title: About DnDPlanner
about.lead: A tool built by and for game masters who are tired of juggling loose sheets and notebook scribbles.

sections.heading: The idea
sections.body: DnDPlanner started as a final-year project with a simple question: why isn't there a comfortable, visual, free campaign planner for tabletop RPGs? The project aims to fill that gap with a modular approach: chapters, canvas events, grid maps, character sheets and collaborative notes — all in one page.

sections.heading: What it's for
body: Preparing whole chapters before the session, with tree-shaped events and spoilable notes the DM reveals at will.
Designing tactical maps per chapter and reusing creatures from the official D&D 5e SRD.
Sharing the campaign with players via a link so each one has their own sheet.
Iterating without fear: undo / redo, autosave and local persistence.

sections.heading: Philosophy
sections.body: No surprises, no hidden fees. Everything is stored in your browser first; once the app is hosted you'll also be able to sync to your account. What you see is what you get.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: Found a bug, have a feature idea, or just want to say hi? Happy to hear from you.

sections.heading: Quick channels
body: Email: hello@dndplanner.example
Discord: dndplanner#0001
GitHub Issues: github.com/USER/AROProyectoFinDeGrado2026

sections.heading: Response time
sections.body: This is an academic project maintained by a single person, so don't expect instant replies. I usually answer within 2-5 days.

contact.thanks: Got it! I'll reply as soon as I can.

news.title: News
news.lead: Recent meaningful changes and what's cooking for upcoming versions.

sections.heading: May 2026 — v0.4
body: Per-chapter map persistence with autosave.
Events editor with backend-aligned types (8 categories) and ||spoiler|| support.
Editable profile: name, description, avatar plus reordering / deletion of campaigns with confirmation.
Full responsive design (768 px and 360 px).
Static footer pages (the one you're reading).

sections.heading: April 2026 — v0.3
body: Square folder in /chapter for better map readability.
Multiple attacks open at once on the character sheet.
Hidden number-input spinners so 3-digit values aren't clipped.

sections.heading: Next up
sections.body: Online persistence (account + sync), robust offline-first mode, PDF export of chapters.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.lead: This is an academic project (final degree work) and is offered as-is, without warranties. The conditions below are indicative; they are not a substitute for professional legal advice.

sections.heading: 1. Acceptance
sections.body: By accessing DnDPlanner you accept these terms. If you don't agree with any of them, stop using the app.

sections.heading: 2. Accounts
sections.body: Accounts are personal. You're responsible for keeping your password secret. Don't share credentials.

sections.heading: 3. Content
sections.body: Whatever you create (campaigns, characters, annotations) is yours. You grant us a limited licence to store it and show it back to you in the app.

sections.heading: 4. Allowed use
body: Don't upload illegal, offensive or third-party-infringing content.
Don't automate mass requests or try to break the system.
Respect SRD 5.1 when using content derived from it.

sections.heading: 5. Changes
sections.body: We may update these terms. We'll announce relevant changes on the News page.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.lead: What we store, where, and why. No useless small print.

sections.heading: Data we keep
body: Your username, email and description if you fill them in.
The campaigns, chapters, maps and characters you create.
The annotations you or your players write per campaign.

sections.heading: Where it lives
sections.body: For now everything lives in your browser's localStorage. When we connect a backend (for example on DigitalOcean) the data will sync to a database with TLS in transit.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: We only use a technical cookie as a session backup. No tracking or advertising cookies.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You can export and delete your data at any time from your profile. For any personal-data request, reach out via Contact.

api.title: API
api.lead: If you'd like to integrate DnDPlanner with other tools, this section walks you through the public endpoints and how to authenticate.

sections.heading: Status
sections.body: The REST API is implemented in /backend using Express + Mongoose. It's not public yet; this page describes what will be exposed in the next version.

sections.heading: Main endpoints
body: GET /api/health — health check
POST /api/auth/login — email + password authentication
GET /api/campaigns — list user campaigns
GET /api/campaigns/:id/chapters — chapters of a campaign
POST /api/campaigns/:id/events — create an event
GET /api/dnd/monsters — gateway to the SRD 5.1

sections.heading: Authentication
sections.body: JWT token in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. Refresh tokens last 30 days by default and rotate on each use.

sections.heading: Rate limiting
sections.body: 100 requests per IP every 15 minutes against `/api/*`. If you need more for a legitimate integration, get in touch.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.lead: What's done, what's in progress and what's coming. The roadmap is indicative and may change.

sections.heading: Done ✓
body: Canvas events editor with connections and redaction.
Tactical map with grid, terrain and SRD entities.
DnD 5e character sheet with classes and API monsters.
Collaborative annotations with spoilers.
Responsive design 768 / 360 px.
DigitalOcean App Platform deployment documented.

sections.heading: In progress
body: Express + Mongo backend wired to the frontend.
Offline-first sync with conflict resolution.
Multi-level map editor (e.g. dungeon with floors).

sections.heading: Planned
body: Chapter export to printable PDF.
Character import from D&D Beyond.
Live mode during the session with virtual dice.
Community-built template marketplace.
